event_text: Malcolm II becomes King of Alba and later helps consolidate royal power.
Macbeth becomes king after the death of Duncan I.
Malcolm III defeats Macbeth and later becomes king.
William the Conqueror invades Scotland and meets Malcolm III at Abernethy.
David I becomes king and encourages major religious and administrative reforms.
Scottish forces are defeated at the Battle of the Standard.
William the Lion becomes king of Scotland.
William the Lion is captured at Alnwick and forced to accept the Treaty of Falaise.
Richard I releases Scotland from the Treaty of Falaise through the Quitclaim of Canterbury.
Alexander III becomes king of Scotland.
The Battle of Largs checks Norwegian influence in western Scotland.
The Treaty of Perth transfers the Hebrides and Isle of Man from Norway to Scotland.
Alexander III dies, creating a succession crisis.
Margaret, Maid of Norway dies, leaving the Scottish throne disputed.
Edward I captures Berwick, beginning a major English invasion of Scotland.
William Wallace and Andrew Moray defeat English forces at Stirling Bridge.
William Wallace is executed in London.
Robert the Bruce is crowned King of Scots.
Robert the Bruce defeats Edward II at the Battle of Bannockburn.
The Declaration of Arbroath asserts Scottish independence.
The Treaty of Edinburgh Northampton recognises Scottish independence.
Robert II becomes the first Stewart king of Scotland.
James I becomes king but spends years as a prisoner in England.
James I is assassinated at Perth.
James II dies during the siege of Roxburgh Castle.
James IV becomes king after the death of James III at Sauchieburn.
James IV marries Margaret Tudor, linking the Scottish and English royal lines.
James IV is killed at the Battle of Flodden.
Mary, Queen of Scots becomes queen as an infant.
The Scots are defeated by English forces at Pinkie Cleugh.
The Scottish Reformation Parliament rejects papal authority.
Mary, Queen of Scots returns to Scotland from France.
Mary, Queen of Scots is forced to abdicate and James VI becomes king.
The Ruthven Raid temporarily captures King James VI.
James VI of Scotland becomes James I of England in the Union of the Crowns.
The National Covenant is signed in opposition to Charles I’s religious policies.
Montrose wins at Tippermuir, opening a major royalist campaign in Scotland.
Charles II is crowned King of Scots at Scone.
Scotland is occupied by Cromwell’s forces after the Battle of Worcester.
The Restoration returns Charles II to power across the kingdoms.
The Claim of Right confirms limits on royal authority in Scotland.
The Massacre of Glencoe takes place in the Highlands.
The Bank of Scotland is founded.
The first Darien expedition leaves Leith, beginning the scheme that later fails and damages Scotland’s economy.
The Acts of Union create the Kingdom of Great Britain.
The Jacobite rising of 1715 attempts to restore the Stuart line.
Charles Edward Stuart raises his standard at Glenfinnan, beginning the Jacobite rising of 1745.
The Battle of Culloden ends major Jacobite military resistance.
Robert Burns is born in Alloway.
The New Town of Edinburgh is planned, becoming a major Enlightenment project.
The Encyclopaedia Britannica is first published in Edinburgh.
Adam Smith publishes The Wealth of Nations.
The Royal Society of Edinburgh is founded.
The Edinburgh Review begins publication.
Henry Bell’s Comet begins steamship passenger service on the Clyde.
George IV visits Scotland, the first reigning monarch to do so in generations.
The Disruption creates the Free Church of Scotland.
The Highland Potato Famine worsens hardship and emigration.
The National Gallery of Scotland opens in Edinburgh.
The Education Scotland Act makes schooling more widely available.
The Scottish Office is created to administer Scottish affairs.
The Forth Bridge opens, becoming a major engineering landmark.
The Glasgow International Exhibition highlights Scottish industry and culture.
Scotland contributes heavily to the First World War through military service, shipbuilding and industry.
The Quintinshill rail disaster becomes Britain’s worst railway accident.
The Battle of George Square reflects labour unrest in Glasgow.
The National Party of Scotland helps lay foundations for modern Scottish nationalism.
The Scottish National Party is formed.
Britain enters the Second World War, affecting Scotland through bombing, naval bases, shipbuilding and military service.
The Clydebank Blitz causes severe destruction near Glasgow.
The Edinburgh Festival begins.
The Forth Road Bridge opens.
Winnie Ewing wins the Hamilton by-election for the SNP.
North Sea oil begins flowing ashore, reshaping Scotland’s economy.
A Scottish devolution referendum fails to meet the required threshold.
The Lockerbie bombing kills 270 people.
The Stone of Destiny is returned to Scotland.
Scotland votes in favour of a devolved parliament.
The Scottish Parliament opens for the first time since 1707.
The new Scottish Parliament Building opens at Holyrood.
Scotland votes to remain in the United Kingdom in the independence referendum.
Scotland votes to remain in the European Union in the UK referendum.
Elizabeth II dies at Balmoral Castle in Aberdeenshire.
